div1.entryFree: Resort, subst. visits paid by way of intercourse and converse: kept severely from r. of men, Gent. III, 1, 108. our houses of r. Meas. I, 2, 104, i. e. our brothels; cf. Per. IV, 6, 86. the cause of my son's r. thither, Wint. IV, 2, 57. forbid him her r. Tim. I, 1, 127. what men to-night have had r. to you, Caes. II, 1, 276. she should lock herself from his r. Hml. II, 2, 143. Peculiar passage: of all the fair r. of gentlemen that every day with parle encounter me, in thy opinion which is worthiest love? Gent. I, 2, 4 (cf. the German Besuch for Besucher).
